rules.rule: Price series MUST be represented as pandas.Series indexed by DatetimeIndex constructed with utc=True.
rules.rationale: Mixing naive and tz-aware datetimes breaks joins/resample; UTC is the project canonical timezone.

rules.rule: Prediction market prices MUST be stored as decimal probabilities in [0, 1] (yes/no columns), not percentages.
rules.rationale: Brier score and Kelly formulas assume p in [0,1]; downstream resampling (ohlc) and forecasts expect this scale.

rules.rule: Simple returns MUST use pandas.Series.pct_change(); log returns MUST use np.log(prices / prices.shift(1)).dropna(). Do not substitute one for the other.
rules.rationale: pct_change yields simple returns; log returns are required for additive compounding across periods.

rules.rule: Sharpe ratio MUST annualise using sqrt(periods_per_year) * mean(excess) / std(excess, ddof=1), where excess = returns - rf/periods_per_year.
rules.rationale: Canonical annualised Sharpe formula per Example 2; ddof=1 matches sample std (Excel STDEV) finance convention.

rules.rule: Sharpe implementations MUST guard against sigma == 0 or np.isnan(sigma) by returning 0.0 instead of dividing.
rules.rationale: Zero-volatility and all-NaN windows otherwise produce inf/NaN, poisoning downstream aggregation.

rules.rule: Sortino ratio MUST define downside deviation as sqrt(mean(excess[excess < 0] ** 2)), NOT the std of negative returns.
rules.rationale: Example 3 defines dd as RMS of negative excess returns; using std(negatives) silently changes semantics.

rules.rule: Sortino implementations MUST return 0.0 when downside deviation is 0 or NaN.
rules.rationale: Periods with no losses otherwise produce inf; return 0.0 keeps ratios comparable and aggregable.

rules.rule: Max drawdown MUST be computed as (equity / equity.cummax()) - 1.0, with trough = dd.idxmin() and peak = equity.loc[:trough].idxmax(). Returns MUST be (float(dd.min()), peak, trough).
rules.rationale: Example 4 fixes the canonical algorithm; alternative peak-detection orderings silently produce wrong peak dates.

rules.rule: Brier score for binary outcomes MUST be mean((forecasts - outcomes) ** 2) where forecasts in [0,1] and outcomes in {0,1}.
rules.rationale: Standard Brier definition per Example 5; applies to YES-probability forecasts only.

rules.rule: Brier Skill Score MUST use the realised outcome mean as climatology when climatology is None, and MUST return 0.0 when the reference Brier is 0.
rules.rationale: Example 5 uses outcomes.mean() as default climatology; zero-reference guard prevents division by zero.

rules.rule: Kelly fraction for binary payoff MUST be f = (b * p_win - q) / b where q = 1 - p_win and b = net odds, and MUST be clamped to [0.0, 1.0].
rules.rationale: Example 6 formula; clamping enforces full-Kelly cap and prevents negative (short) or >100% bankroll exposure.

rules.rule: Kelly implementations MUST return 0.0 when b <= 0 and MUST wrap divisions in np.errstate(divide='ignore', invalid='ignore') for vectorised variants.
rules.rationale: Example 6 guards against non-positive odds and suppresses NumPy warnings during masked vectorisation.

rules.rule: Rolling z-score MUST divide by sd.replace(0, np.nan) (ddof=1), never by raw std, to avoid silent inf values.
rules.rationale: Example 7 explicitly replaces zero std with NaN so downstream masks can drop degenerate windows.

rules.rule: Hot-path metric computations MUST be vectorised with .to_numpy() + numpy ops; pure-Python loops and DataFrame.iterrows() are BANNED for any series exceeding 1000 rows.
rules.rationale: iterrows is orders of magnitude slower and allocates per-row Series objects; project pitfall section mandates vectorisation.

rules.rule: Group-aggregations MUST prefer groupby(...).agg({...}) over groupby(...).apply(lambda ...).
rules.rationale: Performance tips section: .agg dispatches to C paths while .apply boxes each group through Python.

rules.rule: Metric functions MUST be pure: inputs are pandas/numpy objects, outputs are floats/tuples, NO file, network, or database I/O inside the function body.
rules.rationale: All Example 2-6 functions take Series and return scalars; keeps metrics unit-testable and deterministic.

rules.rule: Inputs to scipy.stats functions MUST be .dropna()'d before the call; do NOT rely on scipy to skip NaN.
rules.rationale: Gotcha section: scipy.stats returns NaN if ANY input is NaN, silently invalidating t-tests and p-values.

rules.rule: For mixed/object dtype Series use pd.isna; reserve np.isnan for strictly numeric float arrays.
rules.rationale: Gotcha section: np.isnan raises on object dtype; pd.isna is the safe default for heterogeneous columns.

rules.rule: Masked writes MUST use df.loc[mask, col] = value; chained assignment df[col][mask] = value is BANNED.
rules.rationale: Gotcha section: chained assignment silently fails with SettingWithCopyWarning, producing non-persistent writes.

rules.rule: Default risk-free rate for Sharpe/Sortino is rf = 0.0; default periods_per_year = 252. Any override MUST be passed explicitly, and for 24/7 prediction markets callers SHOULD use a market-appropriate factor (e.g., 365 for daily, 8760 for hourly, 525600 for 1-min bars) rather than 252.
rules.rationale: Example 2-3 pin defaults to 252 (equities trading-day convention); prediction markets trade continuously so annualisation factor must reflect bar cadence.

rules.rule: .rolling(window) outputs MUST be handled either via .dropna() or min_periods=1; consumers MUST NOT assume the first (window-1) rows are valid.
rules.rationale: Gotcha section: rolling emits NaN during warm-up; ignoring this contaminates downstream means and z-scores.

rules.rule: Tick dataframes MUST be set_index('ts').sort_index() after pd.to_datetime(..., utc=True) before any resample or join.
rules.rationale: Example 1 mandates this pipeline; unsorted DatetimeIndex causes incorrect resample buckets and resample-order bugs.

rules.rule: Equity curves MUST be built as starting_bankroll + fills.sort_values('ts')['pnl'].cumsum(), indexed by fill timestamp and named 'equity'.
rules.rationale: Example 10 canonical construction; unsorted fills produce non-monotonic equity and invalid drawdown calculations.

rules.rule: News-spike detection MUST use prices.pct_change(window).abs() >= trigger_pct with project defaults trigger_pct=0.10 and window_min=15.
rules.rationale: Example 8 pins default thresholds; diverging values across modules breaks alert reproducibility.

rules.rule: Version pins: pandas ^2.2, numpy ^1.26 (pin <2.0 unless all transitive deps confirmed NumPy-2-compatible), scipy ^1.14, python 3.12. Do NOT use np.cast or np.float_ (removed in NumPy 2.x); use np.float64.
rules.rationale: Version compatibility matrix + gotcha: NumPy 2.x is ABI-breaking and removed deprecated aliases; silent imports fail at runtime.
